profession_discipline: crop tallying, grain allotment, and festival scheduling
public_reputation: Known around Harvest Circle through steady work as a harvest assessor.
appearance: Mariel Crestmark carries the marks of farlan inkshade in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries role-shaped habits that make local pressure visible in the way they speak and judge strangers. In Harvest Circle, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of a harvest assessor in and around Harvest Circle, keeping ordinary obligations from turning into public disorder.
current_agenda: Keep Harvest Circle workable by absorbing local strain before it becomes a visible scandal, shutdown, or breach of order.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the role of harvest assessor credible while managing pressure tied to status, access, logistics, and local reputation in Harvest Circle.

adventure_hooks.summary: Mariel Crestmark can draw the party into a locality problem centered on farlan inkshade duties in Harvest Circle.
Mariel Crestmark becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with spoilage, price pressure, favor feuds around Harvest Circle.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Mariel Crestmark is immediately readable as a farlan inkshade tied to Harvest Circle.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Harvest Circle: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Mariel Crestmark presents as someone shaped by the duties of a harvest assessor and the daily pressures of Harvest Circle.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a harvest assessor, and proof that strangers will not make Harvest Circle harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Mariel Crestmark refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a farlan inkshade too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Mariel Crestmark adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Mariel Crestmark always knows more about Harvest Circle than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a harvest assessor in Harvest Circle.
social_texture: Mariel Crestmark speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Harvest Circle, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Mariel touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Mariel Crestmark when the party needs a socially grounded face for Harvest Circle: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
